Transcrição Completa: Using Loans Wisely with Josh P
[00:00] [aplausos] All right. Appreciate it. Appreciate it. Thank you, Jay. Yes, sir.
[00:05] So, the premise of this is using the loans wisely, okay? Not going and fucking buying watches and shit [risos] Okay. I know we all want to hit the club and pop bottles and all that. All right.
[00:18] So, today we're going to talk about how to leverage your business with loans and invest in assets. Cash flowing assets preferably, but assets. Okay, this not legal advice. I'm just sharing what I've done. All right, legal disclaimers. All right, cool.
[00:35] So, my goal here today is to expand your horizon on investments. So, Jay did a training a couple months ago about investing in S&P stocks. Uh, I know he does some um trailer parks, that kind of stuff. So, I'm going to show you a little bit about what I've been able to do.
[00:52] Uh by leveraging my business. All right, we're all making money and what we do with it now will affect the rest of our lives. So, we want to make the best decisions with the money now and not go buy Gucci slides and do shit like that so that we can, you know, retire early and don't have to keep fucking chasing clients, right? And fucking updating credit cards and payments and all that shit that we have to deal with. All right.
[01:18] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Definition of Leverage"
NÚMEROS: Não especificados
VISUAL: Definição de alavancagem financeira, duas definições principais destacadas
[01:20] So, let's look at the definition of leverage. So, uh there on the bottom, it's use borrowed capital for an investment expecting the profits to be made to be greater than the interest payable. Okay. And then the second one, use something to the maximum advantage. So, that's really what we're focusing on today. All right.
[01:42] So how do we use your current business and current cash flow to the maximum advantage? All right. So we leverage your existing cash flow with loans to invest into assets and take maximum advantage of the government, right? Just like they've been taking advantage of us all these years. Okay? IRS.
[02:01] So some of the stuff I'm going to show you, um, it's confidential stuff, but you know, I trust you guys. I just want to show you what's possible, what you can do with your business, and how we can take advantage of the government because this there's fucking millions of dollars that are out there right now that nobody has claimed that they set aside for people like us to take advantage of.
[02:25] But because we either don't have our business set up properly or we don't have our bookkeeping done properly or want or we don't know about it at all, that it just goes back and into waste. and then Hunter Biden gets it. [risos] All right, so who likes free money? All right, we all love free money.

So this is uh Amanda Stevens working at Simmons Bank. Uh that's a loan to say that it was uh PPP was fully forgiven.

[04:18] This is probably one of the best emails I got. Shout out to SBA. One of the benefits of having your business and books tight, the SBA and banks are there for you to get ahead. Okay, so this is an email that I got in the portal. It said I was approved for 500 uh K for the EIDL, right?
[04:38] Then this guy emails me from the SBA. Hey, you're approved for up to $2 million. Would you like that amount to stay the same? Who would like to get that email? That's how fucking disorganized they were during this time. It was a mess and we can leverage that. Okay.
[04:58] So, I responded cordially and promptly, "Yes, increase the fucking shit bro. Come on, man. What do you think?" All right. So, shout out to Antonio. got that email and fucking two weeks later that shit was deposited, bro, in the account.
[05:15] So, uh, yeah, that's how fucking wild the shit was. All right, everybody's eyes is open. And everybody got access to this, guys. Like, you didn't I wasn't at that time in 2019, I was doing like 3 million a year. Um, and they gave me a loan based on the 3 million.
[05:33] So like everybody was it was just a free-for-all, man. You just had to get the right person, get your documents submitted, and you got access to that. That's 3.75 over 30 years. You'll never see those rates again until COVID 20. All right.
[05:50] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Foundations of Making This Work"
NÚMEROS: 3.75% taxa de juros, 30 anos
VISUAL: Lista de requisitos fundamentais: negócio lucrativo, livros limpos, S-Corp, salário W2, Gusto
[05:52] So foundations of making this work and getting ready for the next recession/outbreak. So have all the stuff ready so that when the time comes, we're not stressing because there were a lot of business owners that didn't have their books, they didn't have their tax returns, they were doing shading shit and they couldn't take advantage of all the offers that the SBA had at that time. All right, so cash flowing business, that means you have net profit reported on your tax returns, your monthly positive on your profit and loss statements.
[06:22] Okay, they do need to see that. They need to see that you're an actual business making money, not just a hobby. [limpa garganta] Paying taxes on your tax return. All right? I'm not saying don't take advantage of the tax code or uh don't use it to your advantage to lower your taxes, but when you pay taxes on your tax return, you're also investing in your future lending because the banks do consider the amount of taxes that you paid [limpa garganta] uh as a lending criteria. All right.
[06:52] S Corp election. Who got a S corp? Everybody. Okay. [risos] Zach. Maybe. [Interação] LLC that has an [Resposta] Yeah, that's it. Yeah, that's that's the setup.
[07:05] So, it's an LLC taxed as an S corp, right? Uh if you don't know what that is, just talk with your accountant. The profits from your LLC flow through your vis your personal tax returns. Okay? It's just a a letter that you submit uh and you you're just taxing your LLC as an S corp.
[07:25] Reasonable salary paid via W2 to you and using Gusto. Anybody use a payroll provider or [Interação] Huh? Gusto.
[07:32] Yeah. So, Gusto is a payroll provider. Makes life simple. Um, you can set up your employees, you can pay contractors. It keeps track of all the uh the 1099s, the W4s, the W9s, the I9's. Uh, pays automatically all your payroll taxes to the state and the IRS. You can also get uh insurance through there. Uh, you can offer insurance to your companies. So, we have like health, life, vision, um, all through Gusto. Super super good tool to use. Okay.
[08:05] and solid clean books that you understand. So, I was talking to Zach the other day. He was like, "Man, bro, we were using Google Sheets [Zach] back in the day." [Josh] Back in the day. All right. SBA ain't talking about no Google Sheets. They need to see a P&L, dog. You know what I mean?
[08:22] So, make sure you got that stuff tight. Get a bookkeeper. I like to use Bench Bench Accounting. They're out of uh Canada. They have their own software and a bookkeeper. Couple hundred dollars a month. And they keep that shit tight. They follow global accounting practices. Uh, and they're just easy to use. Any questions? All right, cool.
[08:45] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Types of Banks"
NÚMEROS: Não especificados
VISUAL: Três categorias de bancos: National Banks, State Banks, Local Banks com exemplos
[08:47] All right, so let's talk about the types of banks. So, there's different levels to banks, different levels to the game, right? Get your BMW, get you a Rolls-Royce, get your Ferrari at different levels.
[08:58] So, national banks, Bank of America, Capital One Bank, PNC Banks, everybody's familiar with those ones, right? Then you got the state banks, Happy State Bank, State Bank of Texas, Simmons Bank. That's where Amanda works that we work with. And then you have the local banks, United Community Banks, Alliance Bank, those kind of stuff.
[09:20] Now the national banks obviously harder bigger multinational conglomerates hard to do business with super uh hard restrictions to get loans you know it takes a long process to get anything approved and the state banks and local banks are kind of where I focus on because you have more pool you can meet the bankers right go take them out to lunch get to know them um tell them you're interested in doing business.
[09:45] Hey, I want to bring some money over and also ask them about the goals of the bank. Some go some banks have appetites for real estate, some banks like to do um you know investments, some banks, everybody, every bank has a different appetite for different assets. So you want to talk to talk to the banker, see what they like to do and then develop your relationship based on that.
[10:08] The bankers are what came in handy during COVID when you couldn't get shit done or it was hard to get uh access to SBA funds and stuff like that. You needed a banker to like submit it through the backend portal. So that's why you want to get them on your side whenever you need them. They'll be there for you.

But she's just checking on me. She's like, "Hey, Josh and Jasmine, that's my wife. Uh, she wanted to check in on you, see how things were going."
[10:45] So, they're like, "You're top of mind for them. You know, you're developing this relationship." We may not do a lot of business with her now, but bankers know that, hey, your business is growing. You're going to have needs at some point, you know, for whatever.
[11:00] Uh, most bankers are not like this. They're not they're fucking you know, they don't care. They just do the minimum and go home. But she was actually my banker from Bank of America. She left there and she went to Simmons Bank. Uh, and then that's when COVID happened and she helped me get my stuff through Simmons Bank. So, if you have a good banker, you'll follow them going from different banks as they're developing their career. All right.

[11:45] So, she's fucking working on vacation. She was on a cruise and she was still working. So, I was like, "Okay, that's badass." Cuz ain't nobody working on vacations nowadays. You can barely get him to fucking work during the week. So, that was good. So, that's a good sign. Okay.

Just fucking say next and go to the next bank. Tons of them out there. Some are better than others. You got to find the one, stay with them and uh see what you can do with them. [12:30] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Types of Bank Loans"
NÚMEROS: $100K por 48 meses a 8%, $200K linha de crédito, 20% entrada, 10-20% entrada
VISUAL: Lista de tipos de empréstimos: Term Loans, Line of Credit, Real Estate Investment, Owner Occupied
[12:32] So, type of bank loans that's available. Term loans. These are just the most simplest kind of loans. You got a 100k for 48 months at 8%. Okay.
[12:43] Then you have a line of credit. Line of credit is uh it's similar to like a credit card, but uh if you get approved for a 200k line, you can say, "Hey, I need 100k." They'll deposit the cash into your checking account. Then you can go to Cabo or whatever you want, right? Um, so that's a line of credit and then you pay back the line of credit and then it gets replenished and then you can use it up again.
[13:08] Uh, real estate investment loan usually 20% down. Uh, SBA can get as low as 10% down if you go through the SBA with their different programs, which we're going to talk about in just a second.
[13:20] Owner occupied real estate loans, uh, which is what I typically do, is uh, 10 to 20% down. And banks love this one because not only are you buying the building, you're going to be operating your business inside the building. Any questions?
[13:35] [Pergunta] What type of buildings are you buying? [Josh] Uh industrial buildings, like light industrial buildings, [Pergunta] like retail or office space? [Josh] Uh it would be considered like the one that we're in right now is like right off a highway. It would be considered like highway retail. Um but just for my type of business, I don't need retail. [Resposta] Yeah. [Josh] Industrials. [Resposta] Yeah. Light light industrial. Light industrial. That's kind of what I know. I'd kind of stick to that. [Pergunta] Multi-units, [Josh] multi-tenant industrial. Yeah.
[14:05] Uh Lee was talking about yesterday, you know, home services like recession proof. So those all those services, they need a place to run their business. Like they need a warehouse or couple thousand square ft. And so, you know, it tends to be more recession proof. That's that's where I specialize in. All right. Cool.
[14:25] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Types of SBA Loans"
NÚMEROS: $5 million máximo
VISUAL: Descrição dos programas 7A e 504 da SBA com detalhes de uso e limites
[14:27] So, type of SBA loans, small business administrations. All right. these type of loans are still processed at a bank. Okay? So, the loans are processed at a bank, but they're backed by the SBA.
[14:42] So, the the uh the most common ones are the 7A loan program. Any business can have up to $5 million in that loan. So, you can have several loans, but they have they have to uh the maximum they could equate to is up to 5 million. Okay? Okay.
[15:00] So, you can use these loans for funding your business, you know, marketing, growth, hiring. Um, you can purchase buildings with these loans. Um, what else it says? Yeah, short-term working capital, refinancing, current business debt. Maximum is 5 million. Any questions on the 7A? All right, cool. Okay.
[15:22] So, um these are the other type of loan is the 504 loan. Um this type of loan provides long-term fixed asset rate financing for major asset purchases. So, if you were to go purchase a building, this one will probably be more favorable for you.
[15:38] Uh so, those two are the main ones that you would um interact with if you go to the SBA, right? And once again, it is still processed through your bank. So, you have the the the uh relationship developed with the banker. Tell them, "Hey, I'm interested in getting an SBA loan. Do you have an SBA department inside the bank, right?"
[16:00] And then ask them, "Are they fucking competent or not?" Like sometimes the SBA loans, they are going to ask for a lot of documents. You need to have your LLC set up, your tax returns, your payroll uh reports, which you get from Gusto, your bookkeeping. Um, and so some of those people that received that, you know, they've never ran a business themselves, and if they're not competent, then they may end up, you know, canceling a loan. Did you have a question? [Resposta] No. [Josh] Stretching. All right, cool. Any questions on that? No. All right.

[17:05] Uh, restaurant revitalization fund. That one's if you own a restaurant. and then uh SBA debt relief and then report identity theft. [18:12] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Triple Net Leases (NNN)"
NÚMEROS: Não especificados
VISUAL: Explicação de NNN leases, responsabilidades do inquilino, comparação com residencial
[18:14] So, yeah, the first thing is triple net leases. NNN lease. Okay. So, triple net leases puts the taxes, the maintenance um on the tenant. That means you get them in there and they have to pay for everything. If your taxes go up, that means their rent goes up. So, you're literally getting cash flow every month.
[18:35] And the other thing that I like a lot is if they don't pay their rent, you just go there and fucking lock the door until they pay the rent, right? And I put a screenshot over there so you guys can see that. You cannot do that with residential. They have a lot of laws. You got to go to the fucking court and get, you know, do all this paperwork and all this shit right? Commercial, click, paycheck, the fucking rent didn't come in, locked. It's over.
[19:00] And then uh you can you know figure it out mafia style you know what I mean like and then it's easy to find professional management companies. There's a lot of companies that are sell that have been managing buildings uh you know for centuries. So it's easy to find those. Um you can also buy a building that has an existing management company. I'm going to show you we did that uh recently. The previous management company stayed on board.
[19:25] they already have a relationship with the tenants and they uh it's just easy. You don't really have to do anything. They just stay on board and take a little cut.
[19:33] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "First Building Example"
NÚMEROS: 5,000 sq ft, $500K compra, $35,000 melhorias, $685K venda, $150K lucro, $300K cash total
VISUAL: Foto do prédio, timeline 2018-2021, breakdown financeiro completo
[19:35] All right, so let's look at an example here. So, this was the first building that I bought. It was 5,000 square foot. Uh it's a office that that we used to use ourselves. Um purchased it in June 2018 for 500K. Uh I spent about 35,000 in uh paint and we did like change out the insulations and had to replace an AC unit.
[20:00] Um operated my business in there for two years and then sold it uh three years. Yeah. And then sold it for 685 um in 21. Okay. So I profited about 150,000 in there while operating my business making money from that.
[20:20] So, not only am I making money from my business, I'm making money from the real estate that my business is operating in. Okay? And so, that's something that I want you guys to think about. I know Zach had said that he's he's leasing an office like in a we we work or something like that.
[20:38] You know, as you guys grow, you may get employees. You know, start also considering, hey, maybe I should buy a building. You know, you can start off with something small. My first one was 5,000. Uh it doesn't have to be that big. You can find stuff like 1,000, 2,000, 3,000 and work your way up, right?
[20:55] You you get in there, you stay there for a couple years, pay the note down, lease it up, or sell it, make profit, go to the next one, right? And you're building your wealth like that. So, I was able to um to sell it for uh 685. The cash to me was uh the profit was 150 plus the the mortgage got paid down during that time as well. So, the total cash at exit was 300K. Everybody good with that? Any questions? Okay.

VISUAL: Foto do segundo prédio, detalhes do financiamento SBA CARES Act
[21:27] All right. So, this is the next building I bought with that. Um, so this one here, SBA was running an offer, 10% down. So, I only put I bought the building was listed for 950. Uh, I put an offer in at 850. They accepted the offer. um 10% down with the SBA loan at 85,000.
[21:50] The offer that the SBA had at the time was they were making six payments. All right. So, your boy got 6 months of free. So, that was the CARES Act that was going on at the time. Uh paying full 6 months off your 7-day loan. So, we got about $30,000 worth of payments. So, I didn't have to make a mortgage payment on that. So, that was awesome.
[22:15] The building's currently about 1.2 million right now. And yeah, that's pretty much it. We we're operating in that building. We were going to do a lease on it, but uh or lease part of it out, but we didn't end up doing that. We're in there right now, and that's where we at.
[22:32] So, that's how you can take advantage of it. So, let's say, you know, you looking at an opportunity. Hey, see what offers the SBA has. Maybe they'll offer that again. You can take advantage of it. All right.

[23:05] Uh, I like that because you can easily put up a metal building on there. Very easy to build. You can uh it's just simple. It's not you don't have need an elevator and permits and stuff like that. Uh you do have to get a building permit, but it's just easier to construct a metal building than a tilt wall building, which is another type of building that you see where they they make the walls and then they tilt it up. You know, those are more heavy heavy con heavy industrial buildings.
[23:35] So, this one just got uh went through the first round of being accepted at the city. That's David there. Hopefully, they'll we would have broke ground and got started building on those.
[23:58] So, those buildings will be rented out. They're probably going to cost about 350 to 400k to build. They'll be rented out for about 5,000 each. After about 40 months, they'll be paid off fully and then just cash flow forever.

[25:57] So this is a report that I get from the management company every month. So, like a P&L, they do everything like they handle the tenants. They just send me this report and then deposit money into my account. It's one of the most passive things I've I've been able to to figure out. You don't really have to do anything. That's what I love about it.
[26:18] Um, they manage the building from the last owner and so it's a little hard to see, but so, but they they handle everything. AC repairs, um, signing the contracts, finding the tenants, managing it, collecting the money, making sure that, uh, all the tenants are paying, and then dealing with the tenants. So,
[26:38] [Pergunta] what fee are you paying for? [Josh] I'm sorry. [Pergunta] What's your percent? [Josh] Uh, the fee they charge me about 300 a month. I don't know what percentage that uh, [Pergunta] $300. [Josh] Yeah, about 300 bucks. All eight tenants. [Resposta] No, no, it's just four tenants. [Josh] Yeah, it's just four tenants. [Resposta] Yeah, [Comentário] that's cheaper than my VA.
[26:58] [Josh] Yeah. Yeah. It's not a lot of work for them, you know, like they have multiple buildings under them and stuff like that. And that's one of the reasons why I was like, "Okay, yeah, I don't want to deal with that. I'll pay you guys 300 bucks."

[27:20] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Key Takeaways - Multi-Tenant"
NÚMEROS: $118K entrada (20%), $150K refinanciamento potencial
VISUAL: Lista de benefícios: aluguéis sobem, hipoteca diminui, gestão fácil, zero risco após refinanciamento
[27:22] All right. So, key takeaways here. Rents go up over time. Okay. As uh you can have leases where every year it increases, you can have um most of the time if the building is mismanaged before then you can once the leases expire, you can uh uh make new leases at market rates. Okay, then you get increased cash flow.
[27:45] The mortgage balance goes down over time. So, uh that profit that I'm getting is after the mortgage is is paid as well. So, that's uh net profit that I'm getting. All right. So, it's easy to find commercial management companies uh rather than other assets.
[28:02] Uh the company that was managing it was uh was with the last owner. Uh I put a 118K down which was 20%. If I refinance the building today, I could get out 150k. All right, that's more than my initial investment and then I would have no money in the deal.
[28:22] Okay, so that that's really the end goal is to like get these assets, refinance them, and then have no money in it. So you have zero risk. Like you got your money that you put back in. You still own the assets, you still have the tenants, and it's cash flowing. Everybody good with that? Lambo. [risos] All right, cool.
[28:45] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "How to Find Deals"
NÚMEROS: Cap rate 7%+, NOI positivo desde dia 1
VISUAL: Logo LoopNet.com, estratégia de networking com agentes, foco em NOI
[28:47] So, how to find deals? So, loopnet.com is a place that I used to to find deals. You can call the listing agents. So, I called this agent on this building here and I got to know him and like we became good friends.
[29:02] Now, if they've been in the game for a long time, they get really good deals or they're they're also managing other buildings that you can get first dibs on before it goes to the market. All right. You want to focus on net operating income to make sure from day one you're making money. Right? From day one you're making money. That's that's super important. We're going to talk a little bit about that in just a second. All right.
[29:28] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Interest Rates Warning"
NÚMEROS: Taxas atuais 7%+, taxas históricas ~5%, últimos 10 anos abaixo de 5%
VISUAL: Alerta sobre timing do mercado, preparação para próxima recessão
[29:30] So, before we go any further, right now rates are high. You guys know this, right? So, it's not a good time to do this at this moment, but there will be a good time that's coming in the future. So, right now, you know, we're north of 7%. But prior to that, we were hovering around 5%. So for the last 10 years or so, we've been under 5%. That's typically where the rates were.
[29:55] So right now, stack your money up so that you can invest in the upcoming recession cuz it's coming. They raise the rates to slow uh to slow transactions. And once transactions get slow enough, then they'll drop the rates back down to increase transactions.
[30:12] And so, um, the buildings that I've purchased, you know, all their rates were like at five or under, but right now, if you were to go do the same deal, because the interest rates are so high, it's it's not going to make the deal profitable.
[30:28] [Pergunta] What's your debt that you looking for in your buy box, man? [Josh] Uh, at least seven. [Resposta] Yeah, seven and above. [Pergunta] You found anything like that right now? [Josh] No, not right now. Yeah, it's fucked up right now. We just have to agree that you have to keep buying. [Resposta] Yeah.
[30:45] [Josh] Yeah. Um so yeah, in times of recessions, there's massive opportunities. Okay. So recession's coming up hopefully or what rates go down. That's all we need to make this whole deal work.
[31:00] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Deal Example - Houston Property"
NÚMEROS: $1.5M listado, 8,000 sq ft, $171/sq ft, 100% ocupado, 7% cap rate, $117,000 NOI
VISUAL: Fotos da propriedade em Houston, breakdown financeiro completo
[31:02] So I went on to uh to LoopNet. I found this this deal right here. Um this is in Houston. This is a little deal review that we can go over together so that you guys can take a look at uh what I look for.
[31:15] Uh so this one's 100% leased, which is good, right? We don't want to have any vacancies ideally in a perfect world. Um so it's about uh 8,000 foot. Uh 1.5 million. It's about $171 a square foot. It's a little high. I like to be at around 150 or less if I can. And then the cap rate, you know, around 7%. Okay.
[31:40] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Executive Summary"
NÚMEROS: Não especificados detalhadamente
VISUAL: Resumo executivo da propriedade com informações sobre condição do prédio
[31:42] So, executive summary here, they're going to tell you a little bit about the building. Uh when you call and speak to the agent, they have to disclose everything about the building. Uh now, whether they do or not, that's up to them.
[31:55] But, uh I like to call them and ask them like, "Hey, how's the roof? How's the AC units? How's the tenants? What's wrong with it? Why are they selling it?" Uh you know, and it's good to go meet them and, you know, build a relationship with them. Uh they I found that they all they're all really friendly uh and they want to make the transaction go through obviously, but uh they're they're supposed to from their license tell you everything that's wrong with it. All right.
[32:22] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Net Operating Income"
NÚMEROS: $117,000 NOI anual
VISUAL: Breakdown de receita bruta e despesas operacionais
[32:24] So net operating income, we can see down there it's about uh 117,000. So you can see they got the gross income on the top. Uh and then they got the expenses. Now out of the 117, you still have to pay your debt. Okay? So, we're going to talk about that in just a second. All right.
[32:45] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Rent Roll"
NÚMEROS: Vários aluguéis por sq ft
VISUAL: Lista de inquilinos, espaços ocupados, valores de aluguel, histórico de pagamentos
[32:47] So, uh when they provide data like this, that means that's a good sign the the building is managed properly. They took the time and the effort to make the listing properly and put the data on there. So, you don't have to there's some places they just put a picture and say, "Call me." You know, that those are super annoying.
[33:05] Um so, you can see what type of um businesses are in there and you can see the rents per square foot. like the rents are super low, but the area doesn't look like it could handle more rent. Um, so yeah, that's that's a little bit about the that's the rent roll is what that's called.
[33:25] So if you don't see that on the listing, you can email the agent and say, "Hey, can I see the rent roll?" And it's going to be the history of all the the tenants and how much they've paid and how they've paid. Make sure they didn't miss any payments. Okay.
[33:40] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Deal Analysis"
NÚMEROS: $1.5M listado, oferta $1.375M, 20% entrada = $275K, empréstimo $1.1M, 20 anos, 5.5%, pagamento $7,567/mês, NOI $9,776/mês, lucro líquido $2,200/mês
VISUAL: Breakdown completo da matemática do negócio, projeção de cash flow
[33:42] So, how a deal like this could look if the interest rates were good. All right, listed at 1.5. Let's say you put an offer in 1.375, down payment of 20%. Okay, that'll be 275,000. And then you take out a mortgage at 1.1 20 years. Commercial mortgages go up to 20 years. Let's say the rates was 5.5%. So that would come out uh for monthly payment at uh 7,567.
[34:15] Okay. Then your net operating income, which is what we saw on the listing, was uh 117,316. You divide that by 12. That leaves you with um 9,766 uh 776 a month. You make the loan payment of 7,567. So your monthly net to the owner could be 2,200. Everybody good? Understand that.
[34:40] [Pergunta] Are you putting your down payment for the cash? You're using your SBA loan or you getting private? [Josh] It could be both. Whatever you you know like we could all put some money together, friends, you know, like I do some deals where like I'll have a friend put a 100, I'll put 100 and then we go buy something together. We don't go BGPs on it or you make them.
[35:00] [Pergunta] Uh, it depends if I'm using like an L LLC already that's already set up. Like what I did with my last deal was I already had an LLC set up that I wasn't using. So, I just gave him shares off that company, you know, to do it like that. I I try to not set up more LLC's cuz tax returns and bookkeeping and all that if possible. But yeah, if you want to set it up like that, then that's another way to do it. Yeah.
[35:25] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Key Takeaways"
NÚMEROS: $200,000 dedução fiscal via cost segregation, $7,000 custo do estudo
VISUAL: Lista de benefícios: propriedade comercial valiosa, refinanciamento fácil, 100% passivo, benefícios fiscais
[35:27] So, key takeaways, uh, commercial property is among the most valuable things to own on earth. Okay. It should be a part of any savvy investor's portfolio. Easy way to get cash out by refinancing the property. So, for example, like the other building that I own already, since I did that deal two years ago, uh, you know, obviously interest rates were low, uh, rents keep going up. I could refinance it, get all the cash out that I put into it.
[35:55] Uh it's 100% the most passive thing that I've done like zero involvement you know no energy don't have to lift a finger cannot be replaced by an app update crypto NFT right amazing tax benefits like cost segregation study which is a study based on what type of improvements that you've done on a building
[36:15] uh so recently I just did one of those cost me about $7,000 uh I submitted all the repairs and updates that I did to the uh the gray building that I was standing in front of. Um and uh they came back with about $200,000 in um uh tax deductions from the cost segregation study.
[36:35] And so you take those deductions spread out over seven years. Um so you take like 50,000 the first year, 80,000 year two, and then something year three and then something on year seven. Um, so you can see there's a lot of ways to leverage leverage this.
[36:52] Um, aim to buy one building a year reasonably and consistent and you can get predictable cash flow each month without hunting new customers. All right, some quick plays that I got for you guys.
[37:05] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Quick Plays"
NÚMEROS: PNC 4.655% savings, Capital One Spark 2% cashback
VISUAL: Dicas de produtos financeiros: conta poupança PNC e cartão Capital One Spark
[37:07] Uh, PNC's uh, doing 4.655% on uh, savings accounts. So, if you got some cash laying around, that's a good little spot. you know, it kicks off a little cash every month.
[37:20] And then, um, Capital One Spark Card pays you 2% cash back on everything. So, I use that for the business. It's a business spark card. We use it for all expenses and they they cut the check to you personally so you don't get taxed on it. So, uh, you can run all your business expenses on it and then send the check in your name and deposit it. So, I'm getting quite a bit of money from that every month without paying taxes on it.
[37:45] [Pergunta] Is there a limit on that? [Josh] No, no limit. Yeah. [Pergunta] I'm sorry. [Josh] Um, yeah, they did change it, but like I carry a balance, but that one is saying pay off full. They may have changed that now. Um, but yeah, 2%, you know, on every on ads, shit whatever, food, any everything, you know. So I run all the we buy a lot of cost of goods sold, you know, for the photo booths, printers, shipping. We pay with that. So it's a good little kickback that I get.
[38:15] All right, cool. That's it. Round of applause. [aplausos] [Voz] Awesome working. All right.
📊 RESUMO EXECUTIVO
NÚMEROS PRINCIPAIS:
PPP Loan: $120,000 (100% perdoado)
EIDL Loan: $500K → $2 milhões aprovados
Taxa EIDL: 3.75% por 30 anos
Primeiro prédio: $500K compra → $685K venda = $150K lucro + $300K cash total (2018-2021)
Segundo prédio: $950K listado → $850K oferta aceita, 10% entrada ($85K), 6 meses grátis ($30K), valor atual $1.2M
Dois terrenos: $350-400K construção cada, $5K/mês aluguel, 40 meses payoff
Multi-tenant: $590K compra → $741K avaliação = $119K valorização, 4 inquilinos, 8,000 sq ft
Gestão predial: $300/mês para 4 inquilinos
Cost segregation: $7,000 custo → $200,000 deduções fiscais
Cap rate ideal: 7%+
Preço/sq ft ideal: $150 ou menos
PNC Savings: 4.655% retorno
Capital One Spark: 2% cashback sem limite
ESTRATÉGIAS E SISTEMAS:
Alavancagem via empréstimos SBA (7A e 504 programs)
Owner-occupied real estate (10-20% entrada vs 20% investimento)
Triple Net Leases (NNN) - inquilino paga tudo
Refinanciamento para extrair capital sem vender
Buy-and-hold com valorização + cash flow
Desenvolvimento de terrenos industriais
Multi-tenant properties com gestão terceirizada
Cost segregation para benefícios fiscais massivos
ESTRUTURA:
LLC taxada como S-Corp
Payroll via Gusto (W2, 1099s, seguros)
Bookkeeping via Bench Accounting
Relacionamento com bankers estaduais/locais (não nacionais)
Documentação: tax returns, P&Ls, payroll reports
Foco em light industrial e highway retail
LoopNet.com para encontrar deals
Management companies para operação passiva
DADOS FINANCEIROS:
SBA 7A: até $5M em empréstimos
Taxas comerciais históricas: ~5% (últimas décadas)
Taxas atuais: 7%+ (aguardar próxima recessão)
Mortgages comerciais: até 20 anos
NOI (Net Operating Income) deve ser positivo desde dia 1
Exemplo Houston: $1.375M oferta, 20% down ($275K), NOI $9,776/mês - payment $7,567/mês = $2,200/mês lucro líquido
Meta: 1 prédio por ano para cash flow previsível
Zero impostos em cashback do cartão (cheque em nome pessoal)
DURAÇÃO: ~38 minutos FORMATO: Apresentação de mastermind sobre alavancagem financeira e investimento imobiliário comercial
